schedule.description: Rockies 3-2 walk-off recap / Imanaga gem wasted
Series finale preview: Cabrera vs Feltner, 2:10 PM
NL Central: wild card is the real conversation

stories.title: Rockies 3, Cubs 2 — Walk-Off Loss, Bullpen Collapses
stories.angle: Imanaga brilliant (5 shutout innings, 7 K), but bullpen surrendered TJ Rumfield's 2-run HR in the 8th for the walk-off loss. Cubs fall to 34-34.

x_posts.label: Post 1A
x_posts.text:
Rockies 3, Cubs 2. Walk-off. Again.

TJ Rumfield's 2-run HR in the 8th erased FIVE shutout innings from Imanaga. The bullpen couldn't hold it.

Cubs fall to 34-34. They haven't won a SERIES in over a month.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW
x_posts.tweet_type: Morning game recap (time-sensitive, required first slot)

research.facts:
Rockies 3, Cubs 2 — Walk-Off Loss
**Decision:** NEW STORY — June 10 game result. First coverage. June 10 pipeline covered the Imanaga vs Lorenzen preview; today's pipeline covers the result.
**Sources:**
- Purple Row: "Rockies late game rally secures 3-2 walk-off victory over Cubs" (June 10, 2026)
- CBS Sports: Box score — Cubs vs Rockies June 10, 2026
- SI.com: "Cubs' Nightmare Continues As Loss to Rockies Drops Them to .500 – 3 Takeaways"
- ESPN: Rockies 3-2 Cubs (Jun 10, 2026) game stats page
**Key Facts:**
- Final: Rockies 3, Cubs 2 (walk-off)
- Shota Imanaga: 5 innings pitched, 2 hits, 0 ER, 7 K — outstanding outing
- Michael Lorenzen (Rockies): 5.1 IP, 2 H, 1 ER, 7 K
- TJ Rumfield: Go-ahead 2-run HR in the 8th inning — the decisive blow
- Cubs record after: 34-34 (road: 14-19)
- Rockies have now clinched the series (2-0)
**Freshness:** Source articles published June 10, 2026 — within 24 hours. ✓
**Why it matters:** Cubs lost to a team that entered the series at 26-42. The bullpen wasted an elite Imanaga start. Falling to exactly .500 (34-34) is a symbolic marker of how far the team has slid since peaking at 27-12.
**Confidence:** HIGH (multiple independent sources confirm score, pitching lines, Rumfield HR)

stories.title: Series Finale Preview — Cabrera vs Feltner, 2:10 PM CT
stories.angle: Edward Cabrera (3-3, 4.99 ERA) vs Ryan Feltner (2-1, 4.22 ERA) at Coors. Rockies have already clinched; Cubs need the win to avoid the sweep against a 26-42 team.

x_posts.label: Post 2A
x_posts.text:
Series finale at Coors. First pitch 2:10 PM CT.

Cabrera (3-3, 4.99 ERA) vs Feltner (2-1, 4.22 ERA).

The Rockies already clinched the series. The Cubs need to WIN this one — getting swept by a 26-42 team isn't something you just shake off.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

stories.title: Matthew Boyd Return Delayed — Shoulder Soreness
stories.angle: Boyd's shoulder soreness emerged during a bullpen session, pushing back his return again. With Horton, Steele, and Taillon all on IL, the rotation can't afford this.

x_posts.label: Post 3A
x_posts.text:
Matthew Boyd's return is DELAYED — left shoulder soreness in a bullpen session.

Horton, Steele, and Taillon are already on IL. Now Boyd pushes back further.

THREE rotation arms lost to injury — plus Boyd in limbo. The Cubs are out of runway.

#Cubs #CubsBaseball #MLB

research.facts:
Matthew Boyd Delayed — Shoulder Soreness
**Decision:** FOLLOW UP — Boyd activated June 9 (covered in June 9 pipeline). New development: shoulder soreness emerged in a bullpen session, pushing his return further back.
**Sources:**
- Cubs Insider: "Matthew Boyd's Return Pushed Back by Lingering Shoulder Soreness" (June 10)
- BVM Sports: "Boyd's Return Delayed: Cub's Pitching Rotation Faces Challenges" (June 10)
- Rotoballer: "Matthew Boyd Hits Setback in Rehab, Will Not Return to Cubs Rotation This Weekend"
- Yahoo Sports: "Cubs Provide Matthew Boyd Update as Jameson Taillon Heads to IL"
- Craig Counsell quote: "We lose a few days from the initial timeline"
**Key Facts:**
- Boyd was activated from IL on June 9 (after completing Iowa rehab start)
- A bullpen session produced left shoulder soreness
- Return timeline pushed back by "a few days" per Counsell; full extent unclear
- Boyd was the planned 4th rotation arm for the road trip
- Current IL rotation casualties: Horton (Tommy John, out all year), Steele (UCL, out all year), Taillon (hamstring, out through All-Star break)
**Freshness:** BVM Sports and Cubs Insider both published June 10, 2026. ✓
**Why it matters:** Boyd was meant to be the rotation lifeline. Without him, the Cubs are running Imanaga, Brown, Cabrera, and whoever they can cobble together from Iowa. This is a genuine crisis.
**Confidence:** HIGH — three separate sources, plus Counsell quote confirming delay

stories.title: Swanson + Hoerner — Double Slump at the Worst Time
stories.angle: Swanson (.180 BA, .139 last 11 games) and Hoerner (deep funk since taking a fastball to the helmet) are the Cubs' two most important infielders. Both are ice cold simultaneously.

x_posts.label: Post 4A
x_posts.text:
Dansby Swanson: .180 BA. .139 in his last 11 games.

Nico Hoerner in a DEEP funk since taking a fastball to the helmet — power gone.

TWO core infielders ice cold simultaneously. That's the hidden rot inside this collapse.

#Cubs #NorthSiders #MLB

research.facts:
Swanson + Hoerner Double Slump
**Decision:** FOLLOW UP — Swanson slump has been tracked since June 7. Hoerner slump noted June 9-10. Today's new angle: combining both simultaneously as a structural problem.
**Sources:**
- CBS Sports: Swanson 2026 stats page — .180 BA, 62 games
- Bleed Cubbie Blue: Swanson stats referenced in June 10 recap
- Heavy.com: "Chicago Cubs Announce Dansby Swanson Decision Before Rockies Series"
- Bleacher Nation (June 10): "Hoerner's Vexing Funk, the Ugly Month..."
- Bleacher Nation (June 9): "Hoerner's Vexing Funk" — started since taking fastball to helmet
- CubbiesCrib: "Cubs' brutal May could be tied directly to slump from the one player fans least expected" (Hoerner article)
**Key Facts:**
- Swanson: .180 BA on the season, .139 in last 11 games (62 games total)
- 7 HR, 28 RBI; was benched by Counsell June 6-7 for mental reset
- Back in lineup batting 9th vs Rockies this series
- Hoerner: Deep slump since taking a fastball to the helmet (timing: mid-May area)
- Early 2026: .291/.370/.449 through ~149 PA
- Bleacher Nation: "power has been completely removed from Hoerner's profile"
- Average exit velocity: 85.9, hard hit%: 27.7%, wOBA: .308 (down from early-season form)
- Note: Specific recent BA for Hoerner NOT confirmed — using descriptive language only
**Freshness:** Multiple sources from June 9-10. ✓
**Why it matters:** Swanson and Hoerner are the backbone of the Cubs infield. Both slumping deeply at the same time removes two of the offense's most important table-setters. This is not incidental to the team's collapse — it's central to it.
**Confidence:** HIGH for Swanson stats; MEDIUM for Hoerner (descriptive slump confirmed, no precise recent BA)

stories.title: 7-22 Since Peaking — The Season Narrative Is Written
stories.angle: Cubs peaked at 27-12, are now 34-34 — that's 7-22 since the peak. Haven't won a series in over a month, including two series at Wrigley against sub-.500 opponents. The excuses are all used up.

x_posts.label: Post 5A
x_posts.text:
Since peaking at 27-12, the Cubs are 7-22.

They haven't won a series in OVER A MONTH — including losses to the A's and Giants at Wrigley, and now getting swept at Coors by a sub-.500 Rockies team.

This isn't a slump. It's a structural failure.

#Cubs #MLB #NorthSiders
x_posts.tweet_type: Bold take / analysis (strong Bleacher Nation angle)

research.facts:
7-22 Since Peaking — The Collapse in Numbers
**Decision:** FOLLOW UP — collapse narrative has been a running theme. Today's sharpest articulation: 7-22 since the 27-12 peak, backed by the "7-22 since peak" math.
**Sources:**
- Bleacher Nation (June 10): "The Excuses Are All Used Up, RISP, Rolison, Rushing, and Other Cubs Bullets" — "lost 21 of their last 28 games"
- North Side Baseball: "Streaky Cubs Made MLB History With Recent 10-Game Losing Streak"
- Yahoo Sports: "Streaking Cubs, having lost 10 in a row, trying to keep things in perspective"
- Chicago Sun-Times: "Cubs done with 10-game skid, but can they learn from losing streak in season of extremes?"
- Record math: Peak 27-12 → current 34-34 = 7-22 since peak (27+7=34, 12+22=34 ✓)
- Bleacher Nation (June 10): "This team has not won a series in over a month, including back-to-back very winnable sets against the A's and Giants at Wrigley Field."
**Key Facts:**
- Cubs peaked at 27-12 (two separate 10-game winning streaks during the season)
- Current: 34-34 — exactly .500, 8.5 GB from Brewers
- Since the peak: 7-22 (calculated from win-loss records, cross-verified)
- Have not won a series in over a month
- Lost at-home series vs A's and Giants in early June
- Now getting swept at home by a 26-42 Rockies team at Coors
- 12 of 14 MLB teams ever with both a 10-win streak and 10-loss streak in the same season
**Freshness:** Bleacher Nation June 10; North Side Baseball recent. ✓
**Why it matters:** This is THE season narrative for Cubs fans right now. The 7-22 number is stark and factually accurate. The "excuses are all used up" framing resonates.
**Confidence:** HIGH for record math (self-calculable from confirmed W-L records); HIGH for "no series win in a month" (Bleacher Nation + context); MEDIUM for "12 of 14" MLB history claim (single source, North Side Baseball)

stories.title: NL Central Watch — Wild Card Is the Realistic Path Now
stories.angle: Brewers 41-24. Cubs 34-34. The division is effectively over. Wild card — not the NL Central title — is the realistic destination for this club now. And that picture is blurring too.

x_posts.label: Post 6A
x_posts.text:
Brewers: 41-24.
Cubs: 34-34.

Milwaukee has LAPPED this division. The Cubs are 8.5 games back and haven't won a series in June.

The division is gone. Wild card is the realistic goal now — and even that picture is starting to blur.

#Cubs #NorthSiders #MLB

research.facts:
NL Central Watch — Wild Card Is the Honest Conversation
**Decision:** FOLLOW UP — standings covered daily. Today's fresh angle: explicit reframing from "division race" to "wild card reality," prompted by Brewers extending lead to 8.5 games.
**Sources:**
- MLB.com: Standings (Brewers 41-24, Cardinals 36-28, Cubs 34-34)
- ESPN: NL Central standings
- Series context snapshot: Cubs 34-34, Brewers 41-24 (GB calculation: ((41-34)+(34-24))/2 = 8.5 ✓)
- Bleacher Nation: "Cubs... 8.0 games out of first place" (June 10, before last night's loss — now 8.5 GB)
**Key Facts:**
- Brewers: 41-24
- Cardinals: 36-28 (4.5 GB from Brewers)
- Cubs: 34-34 (8.5 GB from Brewers, 1 GB from Cardinals for 2nd)
- Wild card: Cubs sitting in the hunt but outside top 3 NL positions
- Division math: 8.5 games with ~90 remaining — not impossible, but requires dramatic turnaround
- Cubs have not won a series in over a month
**Freshness:** Standings updated after June 10 game. ✓
**Why it matters:** Cubs fans need the honest assessment. Wild card is the goal, not the consolation — but it requires the team to turn this around immediately.
**Confidence:** HIGH for standings (MLB.com/ESPN, self-calculable GB)
